lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: How's your dissertation going?
lines.translation: 你的论文进展如何？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I'm proofreading my first draft and will submit it to my professor tomorrow.
lines.translation: 我正在校对初稿，明天就要提交给我的教授了。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Oh, I haven't even started writing mine yet.
lines.translation: 噢，我甚至还没开始动笔写我的论文呢。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: So I'm really worried about finishing by the end of next semester.
lines.translation: 所以我真的很担心下学期结束前能不能完成。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: You mean you haven't even begun yours yet?
lines.translation: 你是说你甚至还没开始写？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: The final draft is due in five months.
lines.translation: 终稿要在五个月后提交。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Of course I've started it, but I can't get to the writing yet as I haven't found enough resources to use.
lines.translation: 我当然已经开始了，但我现在还没法动笔写，因为我还没找到足够的资源。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: So I'm still researching the topic.
lines.translation: 所以我还在调研这个课题。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Maybe the problem is the way you're doing your research.
lines.translation: 也许问题出在你做研究的方式上。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I started by talking to my professor about where to look for information.
lines.translation: 我是从咨询教授去哪里查找信息开始的。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: And based on that, I found books in the library and a lot of reputable journal articles on the Internet.
lines.translation: 根据他的建议，我在图书馆找到了书，还在网上找到了很多信誉良好的期刊文章。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I've tried all that, but don't have enough to write the dissertation as my department's minimum length is 70 pages.
lines.translation: 这些我都试过了，但还是没有足够的素材来写论文，因为我们系的最低字数要求是70页。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I think the problem is that my topic isn't viable.
lines.translation: 我觉得问题在于我的课题不可行。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: And honestly, my professor did warn me at the beginning that I might not be able to find enough material.
lines.translation: 老实说，我的教授在一开始就警告过我，我可能找不到足够的材料。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: But I was so interested in the topic that I didn't let his advice deter me.
lines.translation: 但我对这个课题太感兴趣了，没让他的建议阻碍我。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Well, I suggest you find a new topic.
lines.translation: 好吧，我建议你换一个课题。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: After all, our professors are here to guide us, so it's best to listen to them.
lines.translation: 毕竟，教授是来指导我们的，所以最好听从他们的建议。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: In retrospect, I wish I had listened to him, but I didn't.
lines.translation: 回想起来，我真希望当时听了他的话，但我没有。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: And now I don't want to give up my topic as I've already invested so much time and energy.
lines.translation: 现在我不想放弃我的课题，因为我已经投入了这么多时间和精力。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: If you're committed to your current topic, maybe you could make some adjustments rather than abandoning it completely.
lines.translation: 如果你坚持目前的课题，也许你可以做一些调整，而不是完全放弃它。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What is your topic?
lines.translation: 你的课题是什么？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: It's "Depictions of Femininity in Folklore from the South of the Country".
lines.translation: 是“该国南部民间传说中对女性特质的描写”。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: That's pretty narrow.
lines.translation: 那确实相当狭窄。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: You could find more material if you made the topic broader, maybe by including other kinds of depictions.
lines.translation: 如果你把课题范围扩大一些，比如加入其他种类的描写，你就能找到更多的材料。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Broadening the topic is a great idea.
lines.translation: 扩大课题范围是个好主意。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I'll start by including folklore from other regions of the country.
lines.translation: 我会从加入该国其他地区的民间传说开始。

lines.speaker: Q1.
lines.text: What does the woman say about her dissertation?
lines.translation: 关于她的论文，这位女性说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q2.
lines.text: What does the man say about his professor?
lines.translation: 关于他的教授，这位男性说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q3.
lines.text: What does the woman say about her professor?
lines.translation: 关于她的教授，这位女性说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q4.
lines.text: What do we learn the woman will do to complete her dissertation?
lines.translation: 我们了解到这位女性将如何完成她的论文？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Today, on Book Talk, we're lucky enough to host John Robbins and discuss his new book, Why Americans Are Fat and How We Can Lose Weight.
lines.translation: 今天，在《谈书》节目中，我们有幸邀请到约翰·罗宾斯，讨论他的新书《为什么美国人很胖以及我们如何减肥》。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: John isn't just a respected writer, he's also one of the rare celebrity authors writing about science today.
lines.translation: 约翰不仅是一位受人尊敬的作家，他也是当今极少数撰写科学类文章的著名作家之一。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Thanks for having me, Rebecca, but I'm hardly a celebrity.
lines.translation: 谢谢你的邀请，丽贝卡，但我很难称得上是名人。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: That's very modest of you to say, considering that your four books have sold a total of 7 million copies worldwide, and they've been translated into 12 different languages.
lines.translation: 你太谦虚了，考虑到你的四本书在全球共售出700万册，并被翻译成12种不同的语言。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: What makes people so fascinated with your work?
lines.translation: 是什么让人们对你的作品如此着迷？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Well, people read my books because more than 60% of Americans are overweight or obese, and other countries are facing similar problems.
lines.translation: 嗯，人们读我的书是因为超过60%的美国人超重或肥胖，其他国家也面临着类似的问题。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Basically, we all want to know how to fix things.
lines.translation: 基本上，我们都想知道如何解决这些问题。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: We certainly do.
lines.translation: 我们确实想知道。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I've read your new book, and it's fabulous, especially when it comes to the way you make difficult science easy for laymen to understand.
lines.translation: 我读了你的新书，它非常精彩，尤其是你让普通人也能轻松理解深奥的科学。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: That's no small achievement.
lines.translation: 这可是一个不小的成就。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I'm glad to hear you find my work accessible, because I was worried when I wrote it that discussing the science might make the book more suited for a specialist audience.
lines.translation: 很高兴听到你觉得我的书通俗易懂，因为我在写的时候还担心讨论科学可能会让这本书更适合专业读者。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: My last book was written primarily for the medical community.
lines.translation: 我的上一本书主要是为医学界写的。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: But this time, I want to help ordinary people take control of their weight.
lines.translation: 但这一次，我想帮助普通人控制自己的体重。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: And how do you suggest they do that?
lines.translation: 那你建议他们怎么做呢？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Can you give us the basics of your advice for people who want to lose weight?
lines.translation: 你能给想要减肥的人提供一些基本的建议吗？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Briefly, I argue that every person needs to consider their metabolism and eat what suits their body's needs.
lines.translation: 简单来说，我认为每个人都需要考虑自己的新陈代谢，吃适合自己身体需要的食物。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I don't advocate one single diet.
lines.translation: 我不主张单一的饮食法。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Some people should eat more carbohydrates than others.
lines.translation: 有些人应该比其他人摄入更多的碳水化合物。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: And different people need different amounts of protein and fat.
lines.translation: 不同的人需要不同量的蛋白质和脂肪。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: But you do have some recommendations for everyone, including eating ten servings of vegetables and three of fruit a day.
lines.translation: 但你确实对每个人都有一些建议，包括每天吃十份蔬菜和三份水果。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: We'll talk about those recommendations next, but now we need to take a short break for a message from our sponsor.
lines.translation: 我们接下来会讨论这些建议，但现在我们需要休息一下，听一段赞助商的信息。

lines.speaker: Q5.
lines.text: What does the woman say about the man in her introduction?
lines.translation: 这位女性在介绍中关于这位男性说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q6.
lines.text: What has motivated the man to write his books?
lines.translation: 是什么促使这位男性写书？

lines.speaker: Q7.
lines.text: Who does the man say his last book was mainly written for?
lines.translation: 这位男性说他的上一本书主要是为谁写的？

lines.speaker: Q8.
lines.text: What does the man recommend people do?
lines.translation: 这位男性建议人们做什么？

lines.text: Stress is often depicted as negative, but research shows that moderate amounts of it can be beneficial for your brain and your body.
lines.translation: 压力通常被描绘成负面的，但研究表明，适度的压力对你的大脑和身体都有好处。

lines.text: First, the benefits for the brain.
lines.translation: 首先，对大脑的好处。

lines.text: Studies have shown that short periods of stress can actually bolster cognitive functioning.
lines.translation: 研究表明，短时间的压力实际上可以增强认知功能。

lines.text: Researchers discovered that placing rats in a stressful situation for just a few hours doubled the growth of new brain cells.
lines.translation: 研究人员发现，将大鼠置于压力环境中仅几个小时，其新生脑细胞的增长就翻了一番。

lines.text: The rats also did better on a memory test later on.
lines.translation: 这些大鼠随后在记忆测试中表现也更好。

lines.text: Scientists think the same thing happens in humans.
lines.translation: 科学家认为同样的情况也会发生在人类身上。

lines.text: But how does stress improve memory?
lines.translation: 但压力是如何提高记忆力的呢？

lines.text: It's simple.
lines.translation: 很简单。

lines.text: When your brain cells multiply, your memory can improve.
lines.translation: 当你的脑细胞增殖时，你的记忆力就会提高。

lines.text: Viewed from a biological perspective, this makes sense because animals that are better at remembering dangerous situations can avoid them in the future.
lines.translation: 从生物学角度来看，这是有道理的，因为那些更善于记住危险情况的动物在未来可以避开危险。

lines.text: If an animal encounters a predator and escapes, for example, it's important to remember where and when that encounter happened.
lines.translation: 例如，如果一只动物遇到捕食者并逃脱了，记住那次遭遇发生的地点和时间是很重要的。

lines.text: Experts assert that the same principle applies to humans.
lines.translation: 专家声称，同样的原则也适用于人类。

lines.text: Now, let's turn to how stress benefits the body.
lines.translation: 现在，让我们转向压力对身体的好处。

lines.text: This may come as a surprise to laymen, but experts say that stress can keep you from getting sick.
lines.translation: 这对于普通人来说可能有些意外，但专家说压力可以防止你生病。

lines.text: Scientists concede that chronic stress can make you more prone to illness, but research shows that short periods of stress can actually provide some protection against getting sick because it increases your immune functioning.
lines.translation: 科学家承认，慢性压力会让你更容易生病，但研究表明，短时间的压力实际上可以提供一些防病保护，因为它能增强你的免疫功能。

lines.text: One study shows that rats that experienced brief stress had a surge of immune cell response, which makes the immune system better prepared to fight illness.
lines.translation: 一项研究显示，经历短暂压力的大鼠免疫细胞反应激增，这使得免疫系统更好地准备好对抗疾病。

lines.text: For humans, there's even evidence that experiencing stress before getting vaccinated could help make vaccines more effective.
lines.translation: 对于人类来说，甚至有证据表明，在接种疫苗前经历压力可以帮助提高疫苗的效力。

lines.speaker: Q9.
lines.text: What did researchers discover about rats placed briefly in a stressful situation?
lines.translation: 关于被置于短时间压力环境下的大鼠，研究人员发现了什么？

lines.speaker: Q10.
lines.text: Why do people tend to have clearer memories of dangerous situations they have encountered?
lines.translation: 为什么人们往往对自己遇到的危险情况有更清晰的记忆？

lines.speaker: Q11.
lines.text: What do scientists believe chronic stress can do?
lines.translation: 科学家认为慢性压力会导致什么？

lines.text: For many managers and people who work in leadership positions, dealing with emails is a dilemma.
lines.translation: 对于许多经理和担任领导职务的人来说，处理邮件是个两难的问题。

lines.text: It's likely the unpredictable, uncontrollable and ongoing nature of day-to-day email in terms of volume, importance and urgency contributes to their levels of anxiety and to diminished leadership skills.
lines.translation: 就邮件的数量、重要性和紧急程度而言，日常邮件这种不可预测、不可控且持续不断的性质，很可能会增加他们的焦虑程度，并削弱领导技能。

lines.text: That's because it's not unusual for many leaders to prioritize email management over people management.
lines.translation: 这是因为许多领导者优先处理邮件管理而不是人员管理，这并不罕见。

lines.text: An obsession with managing their inbox prevents them from dealing with their employees.
lines.translation: 对收件箱管理的痴迷阻碍了他们处理员工问题。

lines.text: As a result, they ignore the issues that might only be mild problems at first, until unfortunately, they inevitably transform into a major problem or crisis by virtue of neglect.
lines.translation: 结果，他们忽视了起初可能只是轻微问题的事情，直到不幸的是，这些问题由于被忽视而不可避免地演变成重大问题或危机。

lines.text: As leaders, they are expected to motivate and inspire their team in pursuit of longer-term strategic goals and also, less ambitiously but more practically, to monitor their daily output to set clear expectations and to give regular feedback.
lines.translation: 作为领导者，他们被期望激励和鼓舞团队追求长期战略目标，而且，虽然不那么宏大但更务实的是，要监督员工的日常产出，设定明确的预期并给予定期的反馈。

lines.text: When presented with a choice between the appeal of their inbox and other more important activities, many sacrifice the latter.
lines.translation: 当面临收件箱的吸引力和其他更重要的活动之间的选择时，许多人牺牲了后者。

lines.text: Daily email demands have a negative impact on their goal progress.
lines.translation: 日常邮件需求对他们的目标进展有负面影响。

lines.text: This is because leaders must divert resources from other tasks to check, filter and respond to emails.
lines.translation: 这是因为领导者必须从其他任务中转移资源来检查、过滤和回复邮件。

lines.text: The solution is cultivating self-control which is like a muscle.
lines.translation: 解决方案是培养自控力，它就像肌肉一样。

lines.text: It can be strengthened or improved over time through exercise.
lines.translation: 它可以通过锻炼随着时间的推移而得到加强或提高。

lines.text: Some suggestions include: making space in your diary for the only period during which you will be checking emails; setting a timer for yourself so you don't become distracted by your inbox for too long; turning off email alerts so you're not interrupted by them.
lines.translation: 一些建议包括：在你的日程表中留出专门的一段时间来检查邮件；给自己设定一个定时器，这样你就不会因为收件箱而分心太久；关闭邮件提醒，这样你就不会被它们打断。

lines.speaker: Q12.
lines.text: What does the speaker say is a common problem with managers?
lines.translation: 说话者说经理们的一个普遍问题是什么？

lines.speaker: Q13.
lines.text: What may happen when managers ignore minor problems?
lines.translation: 当经理忽视小问题时，可能会发生什么？

lines.speaker: Q14.
lines.text: What are leaders expected to do in pursuing their strategic goals?
lines.translation: 在追求战略目标的过程中，领导者被期望做什么？

lines.speaker: Q15.
lines.text: How can a manager best avoid being distracted by email?
lines.translation: 经理如何才能最好地避免被邮件分心？

lines.text: In last week's lecture, we talked about the problems caused by poor eating habits, focusing on how medical professionals are trying to solve these problems.
lines.translation: 在上一周的讲座中，我们谈到了由于不良饮食习惯引起的问题，重点讨论了医疗专业人员如何试图解决这些问题。

lines.text: Today, we'll continue with the topic, but focus on research from a different field—marketing.
lines.translation: 今天，我们将继续讨论这个话题，但重点关注来自不同领域——营销学——的研究。

lines.text: Now, what can marketing tell us about improving nutrition?
lines.translation: 那么，营销学能告诉我们关于改善营养的什么呢？

lines.text: Well, a team of marketing professors has studied the importance of the visual aspect of food and how that influences food choices.
lines.translation: 一组营销学教授研究了食物视觉方面的重要性以及它是如何影响食物选择的。

lines.text: Those marketing experts assert that the impact of the appearance of food is greater than we might presume, and might hold the key to encouraging better eating.
lines.translation: 那些营销专家声称，食物外观的影响比我们预想的要大，而且可能是鼓励更好饮食的关键。

lines.text: So how important is the appearance of food?
lines.translation: 那么食物的外观有多重要呢？

lines.text: Research shows that just seeing an appealing photograph of a hamburger in an advertisement, for example, can cause individuals to imagine the taste or smell of that hamburger.
lines.translation: 研究显示，例如，仅仅看到广告中一张诱人的汉堡照片，就能让人联想到那个汉堡的味道或气味。

lines.text: This can make them more likely to purchase and consume it.
lines.translation: 这会使他们更有可能购买并食用它。

lines.text: Of course, that's the point of advertisements.
lines.translation: 当然，这就是广告的目的。

lines.text: So isn't that a good thing?
lines.translation: 那么这不是一件好事吗？

lines.text: Well, the marketing professors argued that this is actually a problem because the combination of pleasing aesthetics of and easy access to unhealthy foods, such as hamburgers and pizza, may be contributing to a worldwide health crisis.
lines.translation: 营销学教授辩称，这实际上是一个问题，因为汉堡和披萨等不健康食品的美观性与易得性相结合，可能正在导致全球性的健康危机。

lines.text: In fact, statistics show that 39% of all adults in the world weigh too much, and another 13% are obese.
lines.translation: 事实上，统计数据显示，世界上39%的成年人体重超标，另外13%的人肥胖。

lines.text: Now, as we discussed last time, medical professionals have tried to fight the obesity epidemic by focusing on the nutrients found in different foods, emphasizing things like carbohydrate, fat, sugar and calorie content.
lines.translation: 正如我们上次讨论的，医疗专业人员试图通过关注不同食物中发现的营养成分来对抗肥胖流行，强调碳水化合物、脂肪、糖和卡路里含量等。

lines.text: But they've had limited success.
lines.translation: 但他们取得的成功有限。

lines.text: The marketing professors believe that the real solution to obesity is making food look appealing and focusing on the pleasure of both looking at and eating that food.
lines.translation: 营销学教授认为，肥胖的真正解决方案是让食物看起来诱人，并关注看食物和吃食物带来的快乐。

lines.text: That pleasure can be used as a tool to promote healthy food choices.
lines.translation: 那种快乐可以被用作促进健康食物选择的工具。

lines.text: In fact, the research done by the team showed that associating healthy food with pleasant images, experiences, and emotions lead to greater interest in purchasing or eating.
lines.translation: 事实上，该团队的研究表明，将健康食物与令人愉悦的图像、经历和情感联系起来，会产生更大的购买或食用兴趣。

lines.text: This is a better strategy, they claim, than reminding consumers that a certain food is good for them.
lines.translation: 他们声称，这比提醒消费者某种食物对他们有好处是更好的策略。

lines.text: But are they right?
lines.translation: 但他们对吗？

lines.text: Well, the researchers cited a marketing campaign designed to reverse the sales decline of carrots.
lines.translation: 研究人员引用了一项旨在扭转胡萝卜销售下降的营销活动。

lines.text: The campaign didn't emphasize the carrots' healthy qualities, but embraced their beauty and the pleasure derived from eating them.
lines.translation: 该活动并没有强调胡萝卜的健康品质，而是宣传了它们的美感和吃胡萝卜带来的快乐。

lines.text: For example, the ads focused on the bright, beautiful orange color and crisp texture of the carrots.
lines.translation: 例如，广告聚焦于胡萝卜鲜艳美丽的橙色和爽脆的口感。

lines.text: The campaign led to an impressive increase in product sales of more than 10%.
lines.translation: 该活动使产品销量大幅增长了10%以上。

lines.text: The researchers believe that other companies could bolster sales of healthy foods with similar ads, depicting their products as attractive and a source of pleasure.
lines.translation: 研究人员相信，其他公司也可以通过类似的广告来提高健康食品的销量，将他们的产品描绘成诱人的快乐源泉。

lines.speaker: Q16.
lines.text: What does the speaker say a team of marketing professors has studied?
lines.translation: 说话者说一组营销学教授研究了什么？

lines.speaker: Q17.
lines.text: How have medical professionals tried to fight obesity?
lines.translation: 医疗专业人员是如何试图对抗肥胖的？

lines.speaker: Q18.
lines.text: What can other companies learn from the example of the carrot promotion campaign?
lines.translation: 其他公司可以从胡萝卜促销活动的例子中学到什么？

lines.text: The household cleaning products industry really began to take off in the 1950s.
lines.translation: 家用清洁用品行业真正开始腾飞是在20世纪50年代。

lines.text: Prior to that, resourceful housewives who had grown up in a less prosperous era relied on substances that they used in their kitchens, such as vinegar used to clean windows.
lines.translation: 在那之前，在不那么繁荣的时代长大的足智多谋的家庭主妇们依赖于她们厨房里使用的物质，比如用来擦窗户的醋。

lines.text: Today, the household cleaning products industry is worth billions of dollars a year and is dominated by large global companies.
lines.translation: 如今，家用清洁用品行业每年的价值达数十亿美元，并由大型全球性公司主导。

lines.text: In recent years, however, many countries have witnessed a surge in the number of small businesses and companies offering more environment-friendly cleaning products.
lines.translation: 然而，近年来，许多国家见证了提供更环保清洁产品的小企业和公司数量的激增。

lines.text: And they're doing good business, too.
lines.translation: 它们的生意也做得很好。

lines.text: One reason for this is that consumers are becoming more aware of the environment and sustainability issues.
lines.translation: 其中一个原因是消费者对环境和可持续性问题变得更加关注。

lines.text: They also want to make their home a safe place, free from toxic chemicals.
lines.translation: 他们也想让自己的家成为一个安全的地方，没有有毒化学物质。

lines.text: The third reason is people's awareness of and knowledge about technology has never been greater.
lines.translation: 第三个原因是人们对技术的意识和知识从未如此之高。

lines.text: In the past, people might have been skeptical of a new product claimed to be able to achieve amazing cleaning results, but they now have faith in technology's ability to do what was once thought impossible.
lines.translation: 过去，人们可能对声称能够达到神奇清洁效果的新产品持怀疑态度，但他们现在相信技术有能力做到曾经认为不可能的事情。

lines.text: There is perhaps no better example of this than a patented cloth sold by one company, which remarkably uses only water rather than traditional cleaning products.
lines.translation: 也许没有比一家公司销售的专利抹布更好的例子了，令人惊叹的是，这种抹布只用水，而不使用传统的清洁产品。

lines.text: The company claims its cloth lifts, traps, and removes dirt and bacteria, and considering that people don't see these products in shops, or advertised on TV, or in the press, it has been a pretty steep rise.
lines.translation: 该公司声称其抹布可以提起、捕捉并清除污垢和细菌，考虑到人们在商店里看不到这些产品，也没有在电视或媒体上看到它们的广告，这已经是一个相当迅猛的增长了。

lines.text: Many of the people behind these companies began testing the viability of their products by selling them at local markets.
lines.translation: 这些公司背后的许多人开始通过在当地市场销售来测试他们产品的可行性。

lines.text: Then in many cases, customers are doing the marketing for these products by leaving threads of comments on social media, praising their virtues.
lines.translation: 然后，在很多情况下，客户通过在社交媒体上发表一连串赞扬其优点的评论来为这些产品做营销。

lines.text: There are also TV shows and books dedicated to maintaining home tidiness and cleanliness and the benefits of having a clean home.
lines.translation: 还有专门针对维持家居整洁、以及拥有干净家居的好处的电视节目和书籍。

lines.text: Having a safe and clean home might be one thing people feel they can control in an increasingly out-of-control world.
lines.translation: 在一个日益失控的世界里，拥有一个安全、干净的家可能是人们觉得可以控制的一件事。

lines.text: And, there are social media celebrities.
lines.translation: 此外，还有社交媒体名。一位英国女性因其清洁技巧页面拥有超过200万粉丝而成为社交媒体上的红人。

lines.text: One woman in Britain has become a social media sensation with more than 2 million followers for her cleaning tips page.
lines.translation: 说话者关于近年来的清洁产品行业说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q19.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about the cleaning products industry in recent years?
lines.translation: 为什么新研发的清洁产品卖得很好？

lines.speaker: Q20.
lines.text: Why are newly developed cleaning products selling well?
lines.translation: 一些客户在做什么来帮助推广非传统的清洁产品？

lines.speaker: Q21.
lines.text: What are some customers doing to help promote non-traditional cleaning products?
lines.translation: 把零钱扔进喷泉是一个历史悠久的仪式。

lines.text: Throwing spare change into a fountain is a time-honored ritual.
lines.translation: 把一枚硬币扔进水里，你的愿望就可能实现。

lines.text: Throw a penny into the water and your wish might come true.
lines.translation: 但所有的钱总得有个去处。

lines.text: But all that money has to go somewhere.
lines.translation: 否则，堆积如山的便士、25分硬币和欧元可能会堵塞喷泉的运行。

lines.text: Otherwise, the growing piles of pennies, quarters, and euros could clog up the fountain's works.
lines.translation: 今天我要讲讲这些硬币都去哪儿了。

lines.text: Today I'm going to talk about where all the coins go.
lines.translation: 收集到的硬币可以去各种不同的地方，从喷泉维护到慈善或公共服务。

lines.text: Well, the coins collected can go to all sorts of different places, from fountain maintenance to charity or public service.
lines.translation: 例如，在纽约市，从喷泉和公共公园收集的硬币通常用于喷泉本身的维护。

lines.text: In New York City, for example, coins collected from fountains and public parks often go towards the fountain's maintenance itself.
lines.translation: 不过，那些不介意弄湿手的创业者通常会捷足先登。

lines.text: Though entrepreneurs who don't mind getting their hands wet often get to it first.
lines.translation: 纽约市公园里有50多个漂亮的装饰性展示喷泉。

lines.text: There are over 50 beautiful decorative display fountains in New York City parks.
lines.translation: 公园工作人员每隔几周就会清理一次，但大部分硬币已经被创业的纽约人拿走了，剩下的收集量并不大。

lines.text: They are cleaned by the park's staff every few weeks, but most of the coins have already been removed by entrepreneurial New Yorkers, and there is not a significant amount left to be collected.
lines.translation: 不过，其他城市可能会有更丰厚的收获。

lines.text: Other cities, though, can pull in a much more serious haul.
lines.translation: 以罗马著名的特雷维喷泉为例。

lines.text: Take, for example, Rome's famous Trevi Fountain.
lines.translation: 几百年来，游客们都会从肩头向喷泉里扔硬币，以确保他们会回来。

lines.text: For hundreds of years, visitors have thrown coins over their shoulders into the fountain to ensure that they will return.
lines.translation: 由于扔硬币的游客如此之多，罗马官员每天晚上都会清理喷泉，据报道，每天能从世界各地的零钱中得到多达4000美元。

lines.text: So many tourists tossing coins that Roman officials have the fountain cleaned every night, reportedly getting as much as $4000 in loose change from around the world each day.
lines.translation: 每晚收集的大部分钱都用于为贫困人口经营一家超市，而收集这些现金是一项严肃的工作。

lines.text: Most of the money collected each night goes towards running a supermarket for the needy, and collecting that cash is serious business.
lines.translation: 但并不是每个人都遵守规则。

lines.text: But not everyone abides by the rules.
lines.translation: 2017年，一名54岁的荷兰男子因使用磁力棒从特雷维喷泉偷硬币被抓。

lines.text: In 2017, a 54-year-old Dutch man was caught stealing coins from the Trevi Fountain using a magnetic stick.
lines.translation: 他这样做已经好几年了，估计偷了120万欧元。

lines.text: He had been doing this for years, and had stolen an estimated 1.
lines.translation: 他被逮捕并被判处34年监禁。

lines.text: 2 million euros.
lines.translation: 扔进喷泉的硬币可能会引起什么问题？

lines.text: He was arrested and sentenced to 34 years in prison.
lines.translation: 说话者关于从纽约市公园收集的硬币说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q22.
lines.text: What problem may the coins thrown into fountains cause?
lines.translation: 从罗马特雷维喷泉收集的大部分钱怎么处理了？

lines.speaker: Q23.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about the coins collected from New York City parks?
lines.translation: 我们了解到了关于那名荷兰男子的什么？

lines.speaker: Q24.
lines.text: What happens to most of the money collected from Rome's Trevi Fountain?
lines.translation: 罗马特莱维喷泉收集的大部分钱都怎么处理了？

lines.speaker: Q25.
lines.text: What do we learn about the Dutch man?
lines.translation: 关于那个荷兰男子我们了解到了什么？
